mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces corporate borrowing costs and lowers equity discount rates, directly driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced household income, which suppresses consumer spending and generates negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad-based contraction in consumer demand, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive returns exceed 55 percent in triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle demonstrates resilient economic output that offsets higher borrowing costs, generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Direction flips to negative during tightening cycles with greater than 70 percent accuracy, or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Higher consumer price index values erode real corporate profit margins and force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, resulting in negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive correlation between CPIAUCSL levels and 20-day forward returns persists over consecutive 3-year rolling windows with p-value less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls growth exceeding 150,000 in a single month confirms robust economic expansion and strong labor demand, boosting investor confidence and driving positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Returns are negative in more than 55 percent of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150,000, or signal loses predictive power after 2008.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between stagnant quarterly GDP growth and falling monthly retail sales signals weakening consumer demand relative to aggregate output, forecasting negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Divergence metric shows no predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 in out-of-sample testing, or positive returns dominate triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate increases during a high-inflation regime trigger immediate equity sell-offs as discount rates rise and recession probabilities mount, producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive returns dominate during rate hikes when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5 percent year-over-year, or Rank IC remains negative over 5-year windows.
